title: Bytes, Text I/O, Encodings, Newlines, and Cleanup · Program Brief: Newline Policy
summary: A Bookbot relies on the platform default encoding, silently replaces invalid bytes, translates newlines differently by platform, reads the entire file twice, and leaks a handle on failure. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the newline policy increment in explicit text-I/O boundary. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Test LF, CRLF, CR, final-newline, blank-line, and universal-newline behavior without changing semantic counts accidentally.
Select strict rejection, replacement, ignore, or surrogate handling from the stakeholder evidence duty and expose data loss.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Predict Newline Policy · Theory Newline Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Bookbot team handles case 8500 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-text-io-encoding-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-text-io-encoding-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_text_io_encoding_4(byte_count: int = 120, decoded_characters: int = 112, encoding: str = "utf-8", decode_errors: int = 0, handle_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_characters < 0:
        return False, byte_count, "io-count"
    if encoding.casefold() != "utf-8":
        return False, decoded_characters, "encoding-policy"
    if decode_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors, "decode-rejected"
    if not handle_closed:
        return False, byte_count, "cleanup"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_characters
    return True, decoded_characters, "decoded-text"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Bookbot decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected file, Unicode, stream, CLI, test, package, or release case, then state which host filesystem, terminal, subprocess, Git, install, native profile, load, security, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test LF, CRLF, CR, final-newline, blank-line, and universal-newline behavior without changing semantic counts accidentally.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Newline Policy model · Theory Newline Policy, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Newline Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Newline Policy model · Theory Newline Policy, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 6931 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode bytes under an explicit UTF-8 and cleanup contract without hiding malformed input; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test LF, CRLF, CR, final-newline, blank-line, and universal-newline behavior without changing semantic counts accidentally.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Newline Policy model · Theory Newline Policy, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: splitlines and split on newline always return the same records.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-newline-policy-179h7e.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-newline-policy-179h7e(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_newline_policy_179h7e\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?byte_count < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?encoding\.casefold)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?decode_errors > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not handle_closed)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
